description: In November 2018, over 20,000 Google employees walked out to protest the company's handling of sexual misconduct allegations against high-level executives and the resulting golden parachute packages. The event forced Google to end mandatory arbitration for sexual harassment cases.

key_events.description: New York Times report publishes details on payouts to executives accused of misconduct.
Global walkout occurs across Google offices worldwide.
Google CEO Sundar Pichai announces policy changes regarding forced arbitration.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling internal corporate activism and its impact on policy change.
